title: trigger validate (Field)
text: trigger validate (Field) System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified field. Declaration: trigger validate Applies to: Field Activation: Activated during explicit or implicit validation: Explicitly, by validation statements ( validate , validateocc , validatekey , or validatefield ) Implicitly, during a store statement. Implicitly, after the declarative checks, and in forms, prior to the field-level loseFocus trigger. Default behavior: If no ProcScript is present in this trigger, there is no action; $status , $fieldvalidation , and $fieldcheck are set to 0. Behavior upon completion: If $status is less than zero, validation is considered to have failed and the field-level error trigger is activated. If it is greater than or equal to zero, validation is considered to have completed successfully. Description The field-level validate trigger defines the procedural checks that are done to validate a modified field. Validation is performed only if the field is marked as modified ( $fieldmod is 1) and still requires validation ( $fieldvalidation is 1) or if $fieldcheck is 1. Because the validation statements ( validatefield , validatekey , validateocc , and validate ) activate the validation triggers (validate and validateKey), these statements should be used with caution within the validation triggers. CAUTION: The field-level validate trigger should not be used to modify the value being validated, only to check whether a value is valid or not. In the following example, a function that validates the bank account number is called: trigger validate ; of field BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER ;verify that the bank account numbers conforms with the standard call CHECK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER(BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER.CLIENT) return ($status) end; validate Copy Related Links ProcScript for Data Validation ProcScript instructions can be used to explicitly validate data, set validation conditions, check modification and validation status, and handle errors. store Initiate validation and storage of all occurrences marked as modified. It activates the appropriate validation triggers as required, followed by the write and delete triggers. trigger loseFocus (Field) Trigger that defines what happens after a user successfully leaves a field in a form component. trigger store Trigger that responds to a request to save (store) the data. trigger validateKey System trigger that defines the procedural checks to validate a modified primary or candidate key. trigger validate (Entity) System trigger for validating entity occurrences.
